key_issues: ADVISORY (MINOR): Part 6 count assertion is `>= 55` while the inventory is exactly 58; the loose threshold weakens its anti-drift purpose. Recommend pinning to `(check-equal? facade-struct-count 58)`.

ADVISORY (MINOR): facade-request-meta reserved-field contracts are loose (client-info/client-capabilities are `(opt/c any/c)`); the envelope stores the raw 2026 client-info value (not a normalized facade-implementation). Acceptable for S1; add a comment so later layers don't assume it is a facade struct.

ADVISORY (NOTE): the 2025 reserved _meta key symbols (progressToken, related-task) are re-declared locally in types.rkt rather than imported from constants.rkt; needs manual sync if the reserved key set grows.

DEVIATION RULING — 58 structs vs ~75-85 estimate: ACCEPTABLE. Request/notification envelope wrappers (Group 5) are represented via params facades + union contracts + the method-keyed dispatch table; no testable divergence is missing.

DEVIATION RULING — subscriptions/listen omitted from dispatch: ACCEPTABLE S1 cut. Its 2026 params wrap subscription-filter+envelope not yet modeled as a single params facade; facade-subscription-filter IS provided. Registering a mismatched pair would be worse.

DEVIATION RULING — read-resource-result next-cursor as 2026-only: CORRECT. Verified spec-2026-07-28.rkt:915 has next-cursor and spec-2025-11-25.rkt:716 does not; the code is more accurate than the spec's incomplete Group-2 table.

DEVIATION RULING — facade-tool-exec/facade-tool-annots renames: REASONABLE. Avoids accessor collision with the facade-tool-execution/facade-tool-annotations constructors; mirrors 003's own tool-exec/tool-annots naming.
